content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

Let f(i)f(i)f(i) denote the sum A1+A2+⋯+Ai A_1 + A_2 + \dots + A_i \,A1​+A2​+⋯+Ai​ and let g(i)g(i)g(i) denote the sum Ai+Ai+1+⋯+ANA_i + A_{i + 1} + \dots + A_NAi​+Ai+1​+⋯+AN​.

Chef creates another array BBB of length NNN such that Bi=f(i)+g(i)B_i = f(i) + g(i)Bi​=f(i)+g(i) for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

Now, Chef has lost the original array AAA and needs your help to recover it, given array BBB. It is guaranteed that Chef has obtained the array BBB from a valid array AAA.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ denoting the array BBB.


Output Format
For each testcase, output NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Note that 1≤Ai≤1051 \leq A_i \leq 10^51≤Ai​≤105 must hold for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N and it is guaranteed that a valid array AAA that meets these constraints exists.


If there are multiple answers, output any.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
It is guaranteed that a valid array A exists for the given B.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the length of the original array A.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers B1,B2,…,BN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers A1,A2,…,AN – any valid original array that could produce the given B.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute total = sum(B)/(N+1) and then A_i = B_i - total. The resulting arrays are exactly those shown.

solution.approach: Observe that f(i) = sum_{k=1..i} A_k (prefix sum) and g(i) = sum_{k=i..N} A_k (suffix sum). Their sum is B_i = f(i)+g(i) = (total sum of A) + A_i. Let T = total sum of A. Then B_i = T + A_i ⇒ A_i = B_i - T. Summing B_i over i gives sumB = N·T + T = (N+1)·T, so T = sumB / (N+1). The division is exact because a solution is guaranteed. After computing T, each A_i is obtained by subtracting T from B_i. The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case with O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        B = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        sumB = sum(B)
        total = sumB // (N + 1)
        A = [str(b - total) for b in B]
        out_lines.append(' '.join(A))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
